findings.description: Off-by-one pagination: docstring says `page` is 1-based but offset = page * page_size, so page=1 skips rows 0-19 and the first page of results is never returnable by any page value; should be (page - 1) * page_size.
Unconditional sort key `u["display_name"].lower()` raises AttributeError on any NULL display_name (SELECTed column with no NOT NULL guarantee), crashing every search() call regardless of filters.
When name_pattern is set, matcher.search(u["display_name"]) raises TypeError if display_name is NULL (None), crashing the request on filtered searches.

re.compile on a caller-supplied (web-facing) name_pattern raises uncaught re.error for any invalid regex, propagating a 500/crash out of search() with no validation or error handling (also a ReDoS denial-of-service vector — defer detail to perf/security critics).

page_size is clamped on the upper bound only (min with MAX_PAGE_SIZE) but not the lower bound: page_size=0 yields an always-empty window while total reports the full count (silent empty result), and negative page_size produces nonsensical slices.
No validation that `page` is a positive integer; page<=0 or negative values silently return empty or off-by-one-shifted windows instead of an error, given the 1-based contract.

deactivate() returns True when re-deactivating an already-deleted user because the UPDATE has no `deleted = 0` guard, so rowcount==1 reports a successful change even when nothing logically changed (idempotency may mask double-deactivation bugs).
departments() can return None entries (NULL department on a non-deleted user survives DISTINCT), violating the declared list[str] return contract.
